exercises.title: Shadowing - Presentation Opening
exercises.instruction: Shadow this presentation introduction. Match the confident tone and pacing.
exercises.targetText: Today I'd like to share our progress on the new project.
tips: Project confidence with a strong start on 'Today'
Stress key content words: share, progress, project
Keep the flow smooth and connected

exercises.title: Shadowing - Transitions
exercises.instruction: Practice shadowing transition phrases. These help your speech flow naturally.
exercises.targetText: Moving on to the next point, let's consider the implications.
tips: Keep 'Moving on to' quick and unstressed
Use a slight pause after 'point' to mark the transition

exercises.title: Shadowing - Questions
exercises.instruction: Shadow these questions. Notice how your voice rises at the end.
exercises.targetText: Does anyone have any questions or comments?
tips: Stress the key words: anyone, questions, comments

exercises.title: Longer Sentences
exercises.instruction: Break this long sentence into natural thought groups.
exercises.targetText: I've reviewed the proposal, and I believe it addresses our main concerns, though we may need some minor adjustments.
chunks: and I believe it addresses our main concerns,
though we may need some minor adjustments.
tips: Pause after each comma to mark thought groups
Stress the main ideas: reviewed, believe, addresses, concerns, adjustments

exercises.title: Statement vs Question
exercises.instruction: Notice the difference in intonation between statements and questions.
exercises.targetText: The meeting is at three. Is the meeting at three?
tips: Statement: Voice falls at the end ↘
Question: Voice rises at the end ↗
Same words, different meaning through intonation
